summary: iter249 security hardening verified: print-queue agent-key gate, /api/system/invoice-settings auth, global security headers, callcenter webhook secret + full iter247/248 regression. 35/35 pytest cases passed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is enormous (~24k+ lines) — should be split into modular routers; only review-related changes confirmed working.
Security headers added as middleware on every API response (positive); confirm CSP is intentionally omitted (not required for this iter).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter249 covered: (1) print-queue key gate on /pending (GET), /agent-status (GET), /{id}/complete (PUT), /{id}/failed (PUT) — accepts ?key= and X-Print-Key header; (2) GET /api/system/invoice-settings now auth-required; (3) middleware adds X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options=SAMEORIGIN, Referrer-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, Permissions-Policy on all /api responses; (4) /api/callcenter/webhook still requires X-Webhook-Secret. All iter247/248 regression (register/login/inventory/notifications/init-db/customer-menu) and admin staff read access (products/categories/orders/employees/drivers/branches/dashboard-stats) re-verified. Test file path: /app/backend/tests/test_security_hardening_iter249.py.
